title: Bridge Essay: Obesity as a Neuroendocrine Disorder.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Water/Pleasure** (Phase 5) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Water/Pleasure to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Obesity as a Neuroendocrine Disorder.** — Arch Med Res. PMID: 37945442. DOI: 10.1016/j.arcmed.2023.102896. Obesity is one of the most prevalent diseases in the world. Based on hundreds of clinical and basic investigations, its etiopathogenesis goes beyond the simple imbalance between energy intake and expenditure. The center of the regulation of appetite and satiety lies in the nuclei of the hypothalamus where peripheral signals derived from adipose tissue (e.g., leptin), the gastrointestinal tract, the pancreas, and other brain structures, arrive.
